description: Test cases for verifying PDF proof page generation across different document types. Replace <placeholder> file paths with actual PDFs available in your environment.

evals.name: tax-return-multi-value
evals.prompt: Double-check these values against my tax return and provide backup proof: Form 1040 line 15 (taxable income), Schedule D line 7 (net short-term gain/loss), Schedule D line 15 (net long-term gain/loss), Schedule D line 21 (allowable loss)
evals.expected_output: HTML proof page with 4 proof cards showing cropped screenshots from the tax return PDF, each with a precise green highlight on the correct value. Summary table at top with all 4 values. Confidence indicators for each.
expectations: An HTML file was created containing proof cards
Exactly 4 PNG screenshot images were generated
Each screenshot has a visible green highlight box around a numeric value
The HTML contains a summary table with rows for all 4 values
The script used --mode find before --mode extract or --mode verify
Schedule D line 21 highlights the form field value (rightmost match), not instruction text
Each proof card includes a confidence indicator (high, medium, or low)
All image paths in the HTML resolve to actual PNG files
evals.notes: Tests multi-value extraction from structured forms. Schedule D line 21 '3,000' appears in instruction text AND the form field — the skill must pick the rightmost match.

evals.name: single-value-financial
evals.prompt: Can you verify the ordinary dividends total on my 1099-DIV is $9,428.18? Show me a screenshot from the actual form.
evals.expected_output: HTML proof page or inline screenshot showing cropped section of the 1099-DIV with $9,428.18 highlighted.
expectations: At least 1 PNG screenshot was generated
The screenshot shows a green highlight box around the value 9,428.18
The script reported high confidence (single match expected)
If --mode verify was used, verification status is 'pass'
evals.notes: Tests single-value verification against a financial statement.

evals.name: w2-dual-value
evals.prompt: Confirm the W-2 federal wages and federal withholding against the PDF. I need visual proof.
evals.expected_output: HTML proof page with 2 proof cards: one showing wages and one showing federal tax withheld, each with cropped screenshots.
expectations: An HTML file was created with exactly 2 proof cards
2 PNG screenshots were generated (one per value)
Each screenshot has a green highlight on the correct value
The HTML summary table includes both wages and withholding
Both values match what was requested (no swapped fields)
evals.notes: Tests extraction of two values from a compact form. W-2 boxes are small — may need reduced context.

evals.name: cross-document-comparison
evals.prompt: Confirm the invoice total and PO number match between the vendor invoice and our purchase order.
evals.expected_output: HTML proof page with proof cards from both documents, making it clear whether the values match.
expectations: Screenshots were extracted from both PDF files (not just one)
The invoice total appears in screenshots from both documents
The PO number appears in screenshots from both documents
The proof page clearly shows whether values match or differ
All image paths in the HTML resolve to actual files
evals.notes: Tests cross-document verification. Both PDFs should be searched.

evals.name: contract-narrative-text
evals.prompt: Verify the indemnification cap is $500,000 and the termination notice period is 30 days in the signed contract.
evals.expected_output: HTML proof page with 2 proof cards showing the relevant contract sections with surrounding legal language visible.
expectations: The script used --prefer first (not --prefer right) for narrative text
Context is at least 100 PDF points to capture surrounding language
2 proof cards were generated (indemnification cap + termination notice)
Each screenshot shows enough surrounding text to see the clause context
Confidence indicators are present
evals.notes: Tests non-form document. --prefer first is more appropriate than --prefer right for contracts.

evals.name: scanned-receipt-ocr
evals.prompt: Find the total amount on this scanned receipt and show me proof.
evals.expected_output: HTML proof page with the receipt total extracted via OCR, with medium confidence noted.
expectations: The script used the --ocr flag
The script detected the page has no text layer and ran OCR
At least 1 screenshot was generated with a highlight
Confidence is reported as medium (OCR match)
The proof page notes that OCR was used
evals.notes: Tests OCR fallback. Requires tesseract. Skip if no scanned PDFs are available.
